title: Fish Amritsari

For the Marinade: 500 g firm white fish fillets (e.g., basa, cod, sole, or tilapia), cut into 2–3 inch pieces
1 tsp red chili powder (adjust to spice preference)
For the Coating: ½ tsp baking soda (for extra crispness)
For Frying: Mustard oil or refined oil – enough for deep frying
To Serve: Onion rings – soaked in cold water for crunch

Method.title: 1. First Marinade (Base Flavour)
steps: Pat fish pieces dry with kitchen paper.
Mix ginger-garlic paste, red chili powder, turmeric, lemon juice, and salt in a bowl.
Coat the fish evenly, cover, and let rest for 15 minutes.

Method.title: 2. Second Marinade (Batter & Spice Layer)
steps: In a mixing bowl, combine gram flour, cornflour, coriander powder, garam masala, crushed ajwain, and baking soda.
Add just enough water to form a thick, clinging batter.
Toss the pre-marinated fish into this mixture until well-coated.
Rest for another 10 minutes so the flavors penetrate.

Method.title: 3. Frying
steps: Heat mustard oil until it reaches 180 °C (test: a drop of batter should rise immediately and sizzle).
Fry fish in small batches to avoid crowding, turning once, until golden brown and crisp (3–4 minutes per batch).

Method.title: 4. Plating
steps: Arrange on a warm platter with onion rings and lemon wedges.

Chef Tips: Ajwain is essential—it cuts through the richness and adds a signature aroma.
For restaurant-style crispness, double fry: first at 160 °C until almost done, rest for 1 min, then fry again at 190 °C for 30–40 seconds.
Mustard oil adds authentic Punjabi depth—smoke it first to mellow its pungency.
For plating in fine dining, serve on a banana leaf over a stone platter, with chutney presented in a small katori.
